function.name: BLOCK
function.description: block/unblock apps+websites; subactions block|unblock|status|request_permission|release|list_active; web requires confirmed:true

target.description: Which surface to act on: 'app' (phone apps) or 'website' (desktop hosts-file/SelfControl). Inferred from `subaction` (request_permission/release/list_active → website), from app/website-only param shape, or from the user's text when omitted.

subaction.description: One of: block, unblock, status, request_permission, release, list_active. request_permission, release, and list_active are website-only.

intent.description: Free-form description of what the owner wants. Used by the block subaction to extract apps/hostnames + duration.

hostnames.description: (target=website) Public hostnames or URLs to block, e.g. ['x.com','twitter.com'].

confirmed.description: (target=website) Set true only when the owner has explicitly confirmed the block. Without it, block returns a draft confirmation request. Required by release.

ruleId.description: (target=website, subaction=release) ID of the managed block rule to release.

reason.description: (target=website, subaction=release) Optional reason recorded on the rule when released.

includeLiveStatus.description: (target=website, subaction=list_active) Include the current hosts-file/SelfControl live block state. Default true.

includeManagedRules.description: (target=website, subaction=list_active) Include managed LifeOps block rules. Default true.

packageNames.description: (target=app, Android) Package names to block, e.g. ['com.twitter.android'].

appTokens.description: (target=app, iOS) iPhone app tokens from a previous selectApps() call.

durationMinutes.description: How long to block, in minutes. Omit/null for an indefinite block that stays active until manually removed.

function.name: BOOK_TRAVEL
function.description: book real flights+hotels; drafts then requires owner approval; syncs calendar

origin.description: origin IATA 3-letter uppercase (e.g. JFK, SFO, LHR)

destination.description: destination IATA 3-letter uppercase (e.g. LHR, NRT, LAX)

passengers.description: passengers[]: givenName familyName bornOn gender email? phone?

calendarSync.description: calendarSync: calendarId? attendees[]? notes? for itinerary event

function.name: CALENDAR
function.description: calendar event CRUD + availability + prefs; subactions create_event|update_event|delete_event|search_events|propose_times|check_availability|next_event|feed

subaction.description: Which calendar operation to run. Google Calendar: feed, next_event, search_events, create_event, update_event, delete_event, trip_window, bulk_reschedule. Availability: check_availability, propose_times. Preferences: update_preferences.

intent.description: Natural-language description of the calendar request (e.g. "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm").

title.description: Event title when creating a calendar event.

query.description: Search phrase for search_events / travel_itinerary (e.g. flight, dentist, Denver).

queries.description: Optional array of search phrases for search_events. Combined and deduped.

details.description: calendar details: calendarId timeMin timeMax timeZone startAt endAt durationMinutes eventId newTitle description location travelOriginAddress windowDays windowPreset forceSync

durationMinutes.description: Meeting length in minutes. Used by propose_times.

daysAhead.description: Days ahead for propose_times search window (defaults to 7, ignored when windowStart/windowEnd are supplied).

slotCount.description: Number of candidate slots for propose_times (defaults to 3).

windowStart.description: ISO-8601 earliest start of the propose_times search window.

windowEnd.description: ISO-8601 latest end of the propose_times search window.

startAt.description: ISO-8601 start time. Used by check_availability.

endAt.description: ISO-8601 end time. Used by check_availability.

timeZone.description: IANA time zone for update_preferences (interprets preferred hours).

preferredStartLocal.description: Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

preferredEndLocal.description: Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).

travelBufferMinutes.description: Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).

blackoutWindows.description: blackoutWindows[]: label startLocal HH:MM endLocal HH:MM daysOfWeek?[0..6]

function.name: CONNECTOR
function.description: connector lifecycle: connect|disconnect|verify|status|list; registry-driven kinds

connector.description: Which connector to manage (kind from ConnectorRegistry, e.g. google, x, telegram, signal, discord, imessage, whatsapp, twilio, calendly, duffel, health, browser_bridge). Optional when subaction=list.

subaction.description: Lifecycle operation. connect (start auth/pairing); disconnect (revoke + clear grant); verify (active read/send probe where available). status/list are read-only diagnostics for explicit troubleshooting; prefer provider/core registry context when available. Strongly preferred — when omitted, the handler runs an LLM extraction over the conversation to recover it.

mode.description: Connection mode: local | cloud_managed | remote. Defaults vary by connector.

recentLimit.description: verify only — how many recent messages/dialogs to read where the connector supports passive reads.

query.description: Discord verify only — optional search text to prove browser-message reads.

sendTarget.description: verify only — destination chat/recipient/channel for the self-test send.

sendMessage.description: verify only — text body for the self-test send.

profileId.description: browser_bridge connect only — profile identifier within the browser.

profileLabel.description: browser_bridge connect only — human-readable profile label.

redirectUrl.description: google/x connect only — optional OAuth redirect URL override.

subaction.description: Which life operation to perform. Owner-policy verbs (policy_set_reminder, policy_configure_escalation) write to OwnerFactStore.

kind.description: For create/update/delete: 'definition' (habit/routine/reminder/alarm/todo) or 'goal' (long-term aspiration). Ignored for occurrence-level verbs (complete/skip/snooze) and review.

intent.description: Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes".

title.description: Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.

target.description: Name or ID of an existing item to act on.

minutes.description: Snooze duration in minutes (alternative to a preset).

details.description: Structured data when needed. May include: cadence schedule record, kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), occurrenceId (target an existing occurrence directly for snooze/complete reminder mutations), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops).

function.description: owner LIFEOPS verb: pause|resume|wipe; wipe requires confirmed:true

startIso.description: ISO-8601 start. Defaults to now when verb=pause and unset.

endIso.description: ISO-8601 end (optional — open-ended pause if absent).

reason.description: Optional reason ('vacation', 'sick', etc).

confirmed.description: Required true for verb=wipe. Without it, wipe returns a confirmation prompt.

confirmation.description: Alternative confirmation token — must equal 'wipe' for verb=wipe.

function.name: MESSAGE
function.description: primary message action ops send read_channel read_with_contact search list_channels list_servers join leave react edit delete pin get_user triage list_inbox search_inbox draft_reply draft_followup respond send_draft schedule_draft_send manage dm group channel room thread user server inbox draft

subaction.description: Message subaction. One of: send, read_channel, read_with_contact, search, list_channels, list_servers, join, leave, react, edit, delete, pin, get_user, triage, list_inbox, search_inbox, draft_reply, draft_followup, respond, send_draft, schedule_draft_send, manage.

operation.description: Legacy alias for subaction, accepted for planner compatibility.

subAction.description: Legacy alias for subaction, accepted for planner compatibility.

__subaction.description: Legacy alias for subaction, accepted for planner compatibility.

op.description: Legacy alias for subaction, accepted for planner compatibility.

source.description: Connector source such as discord, slack, signal, whatsapp, telegram, x, imessage, matrix, line, google-chat, feishu, instagram, wechat, gmail.

accountId.description: Optional connector account id for multi-account message connectors.

sources.description: Optional inbox sources for op=triage, list_inbox, or search_inbox.

folder.description: Optional inbox folder hint for triage, list_inbox, search_inbox, draft, or respond operations.

target.description: Loose target reference: user, handle, channel, room, group, server, contact, phone, email, or platform-specific ID.

targetKind.description: Target kind for op=send: user, contact, channel, room, thread, group, server, email, or phone.

channel.description: Channel/room/group reference for read_channel, list_channels, join, leave.

roomId.description: Platform room or stored room ID.

server.description: Loose server, guild, workspace, or team name/reference.

serverId.description: Platform server, guild, workspace, or team ID.

userId.description: Platform user ID or stored entity ID.

entityId.description: Person/entity ID for read_with_contact, get_user, or scoped search.

platform.description: Optional platform filter for read_with_contact or search.

thread.description: Optional thread parent reference for op=send.

alias.description: Channel/room alias for op=join or op=leave.

invite.description: Invite URL or token for op=join.

message.description: Message text for op=send or replacement text for op=edit.

text.description: Replacement text for op=edit (alias of message).

query.description: Search term for op=search or op=search_inbox.

content.description: Inbox search text or message lookup hint for triage/draft/respond.

sender.description: Sender identifier for inbox search or reply lookup.

body.description: Draft or response body for draft_reply, draft_followup, respond.

reply.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

replyText.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

messageBody.description: Alias for body when drafting or responding to a message.

draftId.description: Draft identifier for op=send_draft or op=schedule_draft_send.

confirmed.description: Whether the user explicitly confirmed sending for op=send_draft.

messageId.description: Platform/full message ID or stored memory ID for react/edit/delete/pin/respond.

inReplyToId.description: Alias for messageId when drafting or responding to a message.

id.description: Alias for messageId, accepted for planner compatibility.

pin.description: Pin state for op=pin (false to unpin when supported).

manageOperation.description: Management operation for op=manage: archive, trash, spam, mark_read, label_add, label_remove, tag_add, tag_remove, mute_thread, unsubscribe.

label.description: Label for op=manage when adding/removing labels.

tag.description: Tag for op=manage when adding/removing tags.

urgency.description: Urgency for op=send: normal, important, urgent.

persist.description: Whether op=send persists outbound content to target room memory. Defaults true.

limit.description: Maximum number of items to return.

worldIds.description: Optional account/server scopes for inbox operations.

channelIds.description: Optional channel/conversation scopes for inbox operations.

until.description: End date/timestamp for op=read_channel range=dates or op=search_inbox.

function.name: MESSAGE_HANDOFF
function.description: room handoff: enter|resume|status; gates agent per resumeOn condition

reason.description: Why the agent is stepping back (logged in HandoffStore).

roomId.description: Override the room to operate on; defaults to message.roomId.

function.name: SCHEDULED_TASK
function.description: scheduled tasks: list|get|create|update|snooze|skip|complete|dismiss|cancel|reopen|history; kinds reminder|checkin|followup|approval|recap|watcher|output|custom

subaction.description: Which scheduled-task operation to run: list | get | create | update | snooze | skip | complete | dismiss | cancel | reopen | history.

taskId.description: Target taskId for get / update / snooze / skip / complete / dismiss / cancel / reopen / history.

kind.description: ScheduledTaskKind for create + filter for list. One of reminder, checkin, followup, approval, recap, watcher, output, custom.

status.description: Status filter for list (string or string[]). One of scheduled, fired, acknowledged, completed, skipped, expired, failed, dismissed.

subjectKind.description: ScheduledTaskSubject.kind: entity | relationship | thread | document | calendar_event | self.

ownerVisibleOnly.description: When true, list returns only ownerVisible tasks.

promptInstructions.description: create-only: prompt instructions stored on the task.

trigger.description: create-only: ScheduledTaskTrigger object (once / cron / interval / relative_to_anchor / during_window / event / manual / after_task).

respectsGlobalPause.description: create-only: when true, the task skips during global pause.

ownerVisible.description: create-only: when true, the task surfaces in owner views.

source.description: create-only: task source (default_pack | user_chat | first_run | plugin).

minutes.description: snooze-only: minutes to defer next fire.

untilIso.description: snooze-only: ISO-8601 timestamp to defer next fire to.

reason.description: skip / complete / dismiss / reopen: free-form reason recorded on the state log.

patch.description: update-only: shallow patch of editable ScheduledTask fields.

sinceIso.description: history-only: ISO-8601 lower bound on log occurredAtIso.

untilHistoryIso.description: history-only: ISO-8601 upper bound on log occurredAtIso.

includeRollups.description: history-only: include rolled-up daily summary log rows (default false; raw rows only).

function.name: SCREEN_TIME
function.description: screen-time+activity reads: summary|today|weekly|weekly_average_by_app|by_app|by_website|activity_report|time_on_app|time_on_site|browser_activity; read-only

subaction.description: screen-time op: summary|today|weekly|weekly_average_by_app|by_app|by_website|activity_report|time_on_app|time_on_site|browser_activity (e.g. today, weekly, time_on_app)

identifier.description: Specific app bundle id or website domain when filtering screen-time to one source.

days.description: Number of days back from now for weekly / weekly_average_by_app windows.

windowDays.description: Window in days for by_app / by_website summary queries.

windowHours.description: Window in hours for activity_report / time_on_app / time_on_site (default 24, max 720).

appNameOrBundleId.description: App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari') for time_on_app.

deviceId.description: Filter browser_activity to one registered device id; omit for default.

function.name: TODO
function.description: todos: write|create|update|complete|cancel|delete|list|clear; user-scoped (entityId)

subaction.description: Operation: write, create, update, complete, cancel, delete, list, clear.

todos.description: Array of {id?, content, status, activeForm?} for op=write. Replaces the user's list for this conversation.

includeCompleted.description: Include completed/cancelled todos in op=list output.

limit.description: Max rows to return for op=list.

function.name: TOGGLE_FEATURE
function.description: enable|disable LifeOps feature flag; registry-driven keys (flight-booking, push-notifs, browser-automation, escalation)

reason.description: audit reason short

function.name: VOICE_CALL
function.description: Twilio voice dial: recipientKind=owner|external|e164; draft-confirm; approval-queue

subaction.description: Single canonical verb: `dial`.

recipientKind.description: Recipient discriminator: `owner` (escalation; uses owner number env), `external` (third party; recipient name resolved via relationships, allow-list checked), or `e164` (raw E.164 phone in `phoneNumber`).

phoneNumber.description: For recipientKind=e164: destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).

recipient.description: For recipientKind=external: contact name or E.164 phone number. Names resolve via the relationships store.

bodyText.description: Optional spoken message played when the call connects.

confirmed.description: Must be true to actually place the call. Without it the action returns a draft / approval-queue entry.

reason.description: Optional reason describing why the call is being placed (recorded with the approval task).
